source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
Two generator units \( G_1 \) and \( G_2 \) are connected by a 15 kV line with a bus at the mid-point as shown in the <image>.

\( G_1 = 250 \text{ MVA}, 15 \text{ kV} \), positive sequence reactance \( X = 25\% \) on its own base

\( G_2 = 100 \text{ MVA}, 15 \text{ kV} \), positive sequence reactance \( X = 10\% \) on its own base

\( L_1 \) and \( L_2 = 10 \text{ km} \), positive sequence reactance \( X = 0.225 \Omega / \text{km} \)

For the above system, the positive sequence diagram with the p.u. values on the 100 MVA common base is
extraction_notes: image field contains a PIL image placeholder, not a path or URL
raw_answer_text mapped from solution letter A to choice text

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'as shown in the image'
the choices are only labeled as diagram A/B/C/D, so the visual options are needed to select the answer
multi_step: the task requires converting generator reactances from their own MVA bases to a 100 MVA common base
the task also requires converting line reactance from ohm/km and length into per-unit values
the final step is matching the computed per-unit network to one of the diagram options, combining calculation with visual comparison
verifiability: answer is a discrete multiple-choice outcome ('diagram A')
all key numerical inputs needed for recomputation are present in question_text
the correctness can be checked by independently deriving the per-unit diagram and comparing it to the provided options

decision_reasons: Assets are complete: question, answer, and required image are all present, with no registry issues and registry_passed=true.
Initial scoring is strong across image dependency, multi-step reasoning, and verifiability (0.97, 0.84, 0.91).
Although the answer options are image-based, the image asset exists, so the core semantics needed to solve and verify the problem are available.
The answer is a discrete multiple-choice outcome and the scoring evidence states the key numerical inputs are present for independent verification.

variants.title: G1 Per-Unit Reactance
variants.rewritten_question_text: For the system with generators \(G_1\) and \(G_2\) connected by a 15 kV line, convert \(G_1 = 250\,\text{MVA}, 15\,\text{kV}\) with positive-sequence reactance \(X = 25\%\) on its own base to the 100 MVA common base. What is the positive-sequence reactance of \(G_1\) in per unit on the 100 MVA, 15 kV base?

variants.title: G2 Per-Unit Reactance
variants.rewritten_question_text: For the same system, convert \(G_2 = 100\,\text{MVA}, 15\,\text{kV}\) with positive-sequence reactance \(X = 10\%\) on its own base to the 100 MVA common base. What is the positive-sequence reactance of \(G_2\) in per unit on the 100 MVA, 15 kV base?

variants.title: L1 Per-Unit Reactance
variants.rewritten_question_text: Each line section \(L_1\) and \(L_2\) is 10 km long and has positive-sequence reactance \(0.225\,\Omega/\text{km}\). On the 100 MVA, 15 kV common base, what is the positive-sequence reactance of \(L_1\) in per unit?

variants.title: L2 Per-Unit Reactance
variants.rewritten_question_text: Each line section \(L_1\) and \(L_2\) is 10 km long and has positive-sequence reactance \(0.225\,\Omega/\text{km}\). On the 100 MVA, 15 kV common base, what is the positive-sequence reactance of \(L_2\) in per unit?
